# Right PEQ filters
# miniDSP-style peaking filters plus normalized biquad coefficients
# Biquad convention: y[n] = b0*x[n] + b1*x[n-1] + b2*x[n-2] - a1*y[n-1] - a2*y[n-2]

Filter 1: ON PK Fc 193.200 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 1.1915
  biquad b0=1.00183361872 b1=-1.99095135631 b2=0.989276917802 a1=-1.99095135631 a2=0.99111053652
Filter 2: ON PK Fc 190.031 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 1.1828
  biquad b0=1.00181692303 b1=-1.99103747029 b2=0.989374554927 a1=-1.99103747029 a2=0.991191477955
Filter 3: ON PK Fc 252.339 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 2.4906
  biquad b0=1.00114758663 b1=-1.9941644502 b2=0.993288862087 a1=-1.9941644502 a2=0.994436448715
Filter 4: ON PK Fc 1182.046 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 3.4023
  biquad b0=1.00390501417 b1=-1.97514158778 b2=0.97716225651 a1=-1.97514158778 a2=0.981067270681
Filter 5: ON PK Fc 1435.737 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 2.3968
  biquad b0=1.00668327967 b1=-1.95891305851 b2=0.960910441789 a1=-1.95891305851 a2=0.967593721461
